title: Kori Gassi (Mangalorean Chicken Curry)
time: Prep Time: 20 min | Cook Time: 50 min

ingredients.title: For the Chicken
items: 1 kg chicken (with bones for best flavor), cut into medium pieces

ingredients.title: For the Spice Paste
items: 8–10 dried red chilies (adjust to heat preference)

ingredients.title: For the Curry
items: 2 tbsp tamarind paste (soaked in warm water)
2 tbsp fresh coriander leaves, chopped (for garnish)

instruction.title: 1. Roast the Spices
steps: Dry roast coriander seeds, cumin seeds, fennel seeds, black pepper, fenugreek, and dried red chilies in a pan until aromatic (do not burn).

instruction.title: 2. Make the Kori Gassi Paste
steps: In a blender, combine roasted spices, grated coconut, garlic, ginger, turmeric, and poppy seeds.
Grind into a smooth paste using about 1/2 cup water. Set aside.

instruction.title: 3. Cook the Chicken
steps: Heat coconut oil in a heavy-bottomed pan.
Add onions and sauté until golden brown.
Add green chilies, curry leaves, and tomatoes. Cook until tomatoes soften.
Add the chicken pieces and sear for 5–7 minutes.

instruction.title: 4. Add the Spice Paste
steps: Mix in the prepared spice paste.
Cook for 5–10 minutes on medium flame, letting the raw smell of the spices cook off.

instruction.title: 5. Simmer the Curry
steps: Add water or chicken stock to desired consistency.
Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 25–30 minutes until chicken is tender.

instruction.title: 6. Final Touch
steps: Stir in tamarind water for tanginess.

chefTips: Use bone-in chicken for depth of flavor.
Freshly grated coconut gives a creamy texture and authentic aroma.
Slow roasting spices enhances fragrance and layers of flavor.
A few drops of coconut oil at the end elevates the curry to top-restaurant richness.
Pair with Neer Dosa, steamed rice, or Ragi Mudde for an authentic South Indian experience.
